Studying the music you love is a fantastic way to become a better musician. It is more inspiring than dry exercises and ensures you spend your practice time on relevant skills. For instance, you could practice sweep picking for hours, but if it’s rarely needed in your playing, it may not be the best use of your time. By using songs you love as practice material, you focus on what you need for everyday playing. This approach, combined with the ‘100% mindset,’ makes learning songs a fun, musical, and effective way to grow as a musician!

Just Rijna is the founder of StringKick, an online training academy for guitar players. StringKick focuses on helping you train your ears, learn music theory, and develop your musicality, making your musical journey more fun and fulfilling.

items.title: Top 10 drum accessories and tools every drummer needs to have
Top 10 guitar accessories and tools every guitarist needs to have
items.editorNotes: Rewritten for clarity and readability, maintaining factual accuracy and original links.
items.excerptBefore: Discover the top 10 guitar accessories you must have! From picks to metronomes, these essentials will elevate your musical journey.
items.excerptAfter: Discover the top 10 essential guitar accessories and tools every guitarist should have in their gig bag, from metronomes to extra strings.
items.seoTitleBefore: Top 10 guitar accessories and tools every guitarist needs to have
items.seoTitleAfter: Top 10 Guitar Accessories and Tools Every Guitarist Needs
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Discover the top 10 guitar accessories you must have! From picks to metronomes, these essentials will elevate your musical journey.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the must-have guitar accessories and tools that enhance your playing experience and keep you prepared for any performance.
items.beforeBody:
Whether you're a seasoned performer or just strumming your way through life, one thing remains constant: your trusty guitar accessories. These tools are not just simple accessories; they are an essential treasure that can save the day when you least expect it.

So, let's dive into the top 10 must-haves guitar accessories and tools that every guitarist must have in their gig bag.

1. Soundbrenner's wearable metronomes

Core 2 Core Steel Pulse

The Soundbrenner Core 2 and Pulse metronomes are very important tools for serious guitar players. Their groundbreaking vibrating metronome feature allows you to feel the beat, transforming the way you engage with rhythm. In addition, they come with an ultra-precise contact tuner, ensuring your instrument always sounds its best.

The built-in dB meter actively monitors volume levels to protect your hearing. These devices even integrate smartwatch functionalities to keep you updated with notifications. Elevate your music practice to new heights and achieve your musical goals with these multifaceted, state-of-the-art gadgets.

2. Guitar picks

Buy now

Firstly, every guitarist knows the trouble of misplacing a pick. Ensure you have an array of picks, each with different thicknesses, within your kit of guitar accessories. They're your unsung heroes, helping you achieve the perfect tone and feel.

3. Stomp Bluetooth foot pedal

Buy now

Meet Stomp, the top-rated Bluetooth foot pedal essential for all musicians, including guitarists. With its rugged, durable metal enclosure, this hands-free page-turner and app controller improves both practice and gigs. Navigate through chord charts or control your metronome with its 6 customizable buttons.

Offering multiple power options, it runs for 150+ hours on a single 9V battery and even includes a USB port for device charging. With a 50+ foot range and universal compatibility, Stomp ensures seamless, reliable operation every time.

4. Minuendo lossless earplugs

Buy now

Minuendo lossless earplugs are an indispensable addition to any guitarist's accessories. They are the world's first-ever variable passive Hi-Fi earplugs, designed specifically for musicians. The patented membrane technology and micrometer precision craftsmanship ensure there is no loss in frequency, eliminating the muffled sound commonly associated with foam earplugs.

With stepless adjustments, you can easily adjust the earplugs from -7dB to -25dB, allowing you to find your personal sweet spot. The magnetic locking system and an extensive range of ear tip options ensure that you will never lose your earplugs, and you will always have a comfortable, leak-free fit. Ideal for use in concerts, practice sessions, and everyday life.

5. Extra strings

Buy now

Broken strings can bring your performance to a halt. Carry extra sets of strings in your gig bag containing guitar accessories, so you can quickly replace them and keep the music flowing.

6. Capo

Buy now

A capo is your key to unlocking new possibilities on the fretboard. It allows you to change the key to a song effortlessly, adding depth and versatility to your repertoire.

7. Guitar strap 

Buy now

A comfortable and stylish guitar strap is essential for those long jam sessions or concerts. It ensures your guitar stays secure and allows you to play with ease.

8. String winder and cutter

Buy now

String changes become a breeze with a string winder and cutter. These handy tools save you time and effort, leaving you more time to make music.

9. Allen wrenches

Buy now

Guitars sometimes need adjustments. Allen wrenches are very important for adjusting the setup of your guitar, making sure it plays at its best.

10. Jack-to-jack cable 

Buy now

An electric guitar that you can’t plug into an amplifier is basically a heavy wooden fashion accessory. And cables are surprisingly easy things to forget when taking your kit out on the road.

Even if you’re playing a gig or going to a studio where someone else will give you the amplifier, don’t assume they’ll also provide you with a way of plugging in! So, always keep a good and handy jack-to-jack cable to avoid any hiccups during your performance.

Bottom line

Keeping these essential guitar accessories at hand not only keeps you prepared, but also elevates your playing experience. Each item serves a unique purpose and makes sure your guitar and your performance are always at their peak.

Check out our blog for expert tips on improving your guitar skills to make full use of your guitar and these supplementary accessories.

items.title: Exploring 3 basic steel drum techniques
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while maintaining factual accuracy.
items.excerptBefore: Learn about position, grip and stroke. Try out these versatile and useful steel drum techniques for all players!
items.excerptAfter: Explore essential steel drum techniques including position, grip, and strokes to enhance your playing.
items.seoTitleBefore: Exploring 3 basic steel drum techniques
items.seoTitleAfter: Basic Steel Drum Techniques for Beginners
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Learn about position, grip, and stroke. Try out these versatile and useful steel drum techniques for all players!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Learn fundamental steel drum techniques such as position, grip, and strokes to improve your playing skills and enjoy making music.
items.beforeBody:
Steel drum (or steel pans) have been a part of Caribbean music for many years. There are literally hundreds of different steel drum techniques that can be used to add to your music, but not all of them are used by players. Some may be too advanced and complex, while others just don't sound good when applied to songs.

The techniques in this article were chosen to provide the most versatility and overall usefulness when creating music with steel drums. The biggest challenge that you'll run into as a new player is trying to decide which techniques to try first.

The basics

In this post, I will tell you about the basics of playing steel drums: position, grip, and stroke. After that, you can experiment with different playing techniques and have fun with your drum.

It doesn't matter if you have just begun your journey or are someone who is thinking about getting back into playing steel drums. Exploring steel drum techniques will be fun either way.

Position

Finding a position that feels natural and comfortable is basic but important. The very first thing to keep in mind is that you should be standing directly in front of the instrument, with your arms at or above waist level and your hands close to the surface at all times.

This may not sound like a big deal, but it's incredibly important—if you don't get into this position, it will be extremely difficult to play accurately and with speed. It took me a long time to find my groove in terms of where I felt comfortable playing from. Sometimes I was too close, sometimes I was too far away—it took me a long time to figure out what worked for me.

The other thing that people often forget about is that it's important to distribute your weight evenly between both feet. If you're feeling unbalanced, try switching up which foot is forward and which is back. This will help you play more comfortably, which makes it easier to focus on form instead of just trying to stay upright.

Grip

First thing's first: make sure you're holding it correctly. There are many ways to hold your mallet, but I'll go over the most common ways.

Let’s begin with the basic grip. Hold the pan mallet with a fist and press it with your index finger. Hold more than one-third of the mallet with a light grip. This is important when it comes to using different amounts of pressure on different strokes in a roll or pattern.

The next thing you'll want to do is grip the mallet with three fingers instead of just two. The pinky finger should be tucked underneath so that no pressure is applied to it at all. This helps prevent injury and makes it easier for you to move your hand freely while playing.

How to hold the mallet

I found that my wrist would get tired quickly if I held the mallet too tightly. That way, I wouldn't be able to play for very long. But if I held it loosely, the sound wasn't as clear. 

After a lot of trial and error and researching online, I found out that you can't hold it too tightly or too loosely. If you hold it too tight, it will affect your technique, and you won't be able to produce a good sound.

It's better to hold it a bit loose and keep the mallet contact time as short as possible, so the notes can vibrate freely. The key is finding that sweet spot where your grip is just right—not too tight, but not too loose either.

Stroke

Now, when you know how to position yourself in front of the drum and how to hold the mallet, it's time to learn how to hit the drum and produce the sound. There are two main steel drum strokes you need to know: the down-up stroke and the flick.

The down-up stroke

Let's start with the down-up stroke. When using this stroke, you lower your pan stick using your wrist and then immediately return to your starting position. This will let you produce a crisp, clear note. It's super easy!

The flick

The second major steel drum stroke is called the flick. This is where you move your wrist back and forth in a flicking motion. Flick stroke is used to move from lower notes to higher notes on the steel drum in a smooth motion.

With this stroke, you can move your pan stick from the bottom of the pan to the left or right side of the pan. The flick technique is super useful because it helps to create melodic sounds that are pleasing to the ear.

The single roll

There is one additional stroke I would like to teach you, and it's called single roll. This stroke is not unique to the steel pan. I have heard it used in many other types of percussion music as well. The single roll is great for sustaining a tone on the steel pan. Try it out by rapidly alternating right and left-hand down-up strokes. 

You should hit the drum evenly and quickly. If you cannot manage it at the beginning, start slowly. Once you are sure the roll is even, you can gradually increase the speed. 

When I first started playing steel drums, it was difficult to get the right sound. It took a lot of trial and error and a lot of patience to learn how to play the steel drum well. I had to learn how to position myself in front of the steel drum, hold the mallet, and balance my strike in order to produce the best sound. Once I mastered these techniques, I felt like I was able to make better music with my steel drums.

The rest is really all about experimentation. Every player will have their own set of tricks and techniques that they put together to make their sound, so there's plenty of room for everyone. The sky's the limit if you have enough time, patience, and passion.
items.afterBody:

The techniques discussed in this article were selected for their versatility and usefulness in creating music with steel drums. As a new player, your biggest challenge will be deciding which techniques to try first.

The basics

This post will cover the basics of playing steel drums: position, grip, and stroke. After mastering these, you can experiment with different playing techniques and enjoy your drum.

Whether you are just beginning or returning to playing steel drums, exploring these techniques will be enjoyable.

Position

Finding a natural and comfortable position is essential. Stand directly in front of the instrument, with your arms at or above waist level and your hands close to the surface.

This position is crucial for playing accurately and quickly. It took me time to find my comfortable playing position, as I often found myself too close or too far away.

Additionally, distribute your weight evenly between both feet. If you feel unbalanced, try switching which foot is forward. This will help you play more comfortably and focus on your form.

First, ensure you are holding the mallet correctly. There are various ways to grip your mallet, but I will cover the most common methods.

Holding the mallet too tightly can lead to wrist fatigue, while holding it too loosely may result in unclear sound.

After much trial and error, I learned that the grip should be neither too tight nor too loose. A loose grip with minimal mallet contact time allows the notes to vibrate freely. Finding the right balance is key.

Stroke

Now that you know how to position yourself and hold the mallet, it's time to learn how to strike the drum and produce sound. There are two main strokes to master: the down-up stroke and the flick.

The down-up stroke

Begin with the down-up stroke. Lower your pan stick using your wrist and then return to the starting position. This technique produces a crisp, clear note.

The flick

The second major stroke is called the flick. This involves moving your wrist back and forth in a flicking motion. The flick is used to transition smoothly from lower to higher notes on the steel drum.

This technique allows you to move your pan stick from the bottom of the pan to the left or right side, creating melodic sounds that are pleasing to the ear.

The single roll

Another useful stroke is the single roll, which is common in various percussion styles. This stroke is great for sustaining a tone on the steel pan. Practice by rapidly alternating right and left-hand down-up strokes.

Ensure you strike the drum evenly and quickly. If you struggle initially, start slowly and gradually increase your speed once you achieve an even roll.

When I began playing steel drums, producing the right sound was challenging. It required patience and practice to learn how to position myself, hold the mallet, and balance my strikes for the best sound. Once I mastered these techniques, I felt more capable of creating music with my steel drums.

Ultimately, experimentation is key. Every player develops their own set of techniques to create their unique sound, so there is plenty of room for creativity. With time, patience, and passion, the possibilities are endless.

items.title: What is a steady beat? See how it helps our developing brain
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while maintaining factual accuracy.
items.excerptBefore: Discover what is a steady beat and how it can significantly influence the development of our brain in this blog post.
items.excerptAfter: Explore the concept of a steady beat and its importance in music and brain development.
items.seoTitleAfter: Understanding Steady Beat and Its Impact on Brain…
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Discover what is a steady beat and how it can significantly influence the development of our brain in this blog post.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover what a steady beat is and how it influences brain development, coordination, language skills, and focus. Learn tips for developing a steady beat.
items.beforeBody:
Have you ever wondered what is a steady beat? Well, today is your lucky day because we're diving deep into this fascinating topic! 

And guess what? It's not just about music. The steady beat has a significant impact on our brain development, too. 

Exciting, right?

What is a steady beat?

Let's start at the beginning. What is a steady beat? Simply put, a steady beat is a continuous and consistent pulse in a piece of music. 

It’s the heartbeat of a song that you tap your foot or clap your hands to. And it's innate to us as humans. Even babies respond to a steady beat!

The importance of a steady beat

So why is a steady beat so essential? Well, it turns out that it is not just crucial for music. It plays a significant role in our brain development and greatly helps in improving cognitive functioning.

Here’s how:

  • Coordination and Motor Skills: Moving to a steady beat helps develop our coordination and motor skills. That's why activities like dancing or playing an instrument are so good for us!
  • Language Development: The rhythm of our language is closely related to a beat. So, by developing a sense of steady beat, we can enhance our language skills as well!
  • Focus and Attention: Following a beat requires focus and attention. Therefore, engaging with it can help improve our concentration levels.

Soundbrenner’s role

At Soundbrenner, we understand the importance of a stable beat, which is why we have developed products to help musicians stay on track. 

Our wearable metronome, the Soundbrenner Core 2, helps you feel the beat, not just hear it. And the best part? It’s wearable, so you can take it with you wherever you go!

Tips for developing a steady beat

Here are some fun and easy tips for developing a stable beat:

  • Clap to the Beat: Put on your favorite song and clap along to the beat. It’s a simple exercise but really effective.
  • Use a Metronome: Practice playing an instrument with a metronome. It will help you maintain a beat while playing.
  • Dance: Dancing is a fun way to engage with a beat. So, put on some music and dance like nobody's watching!

Conclusion

So, a stable beat is not just the heartbeat of music; it's a crucial element in our brain development as well. Let’s embrace the beat and let it guide us, not just in music, but also in life.

Remember, we’re here to help you on your musical journey. So check out our products and let us help you develop your sense of a stable beat! Now that you know what a steady beat is, this article can guide you on ways to unleash your inner beat and produce more rhythmic songs.

And that's a wrap! We hope you enjoyed this blog post and found it informative. Keep on rocking, and remember – the beat goes on!

items.beforeBody:
Something special is launching...

Are you ready to take your music practice to new heights? 

 

Introducing the all new 2022 Core Limited Edition in Space Cadet Blue. Voted by YOU, our musician community, as our top color choice of 2022. Only 250 units will be available upon its first release. So save the date and watch out for the exciting drop on Friday 15th July 2022, at 9:00AM PST!

You can also sign up to get priority access to the launch, and a chance to win one for free! 

Sign up here

Your favorite watch made even more stellar

A closer look at the 2022 Core Limited Edition

  • One and only watch rig in Space Cadet Blue 
  • Special engraving on the back of the rig
  • A limited edition grey and blue nylon strap
  • An additional silicone strap
  • A handwritten note with your unit number

Soundbrenner Core Limited Edition product features

  • Vibrating metronome - go beyond clicks. It’s time to explore the power of vibrations to guide your inner sense of rhythm. 
  • Contact tuner - stay in tune no matter what space you’re in. The Core watch rig doubles as your handy tuner, simply attach to your string instrument and tune accurately, anywhere.
  • dB meter - monitor your surrounding sound levels and protect your hearing at all times. 
  • Smartwatch - receive phone notifications, access a stopwatch, and even an alarm clock. Now you’ve got all the basics covered. 

What’s more, the 2022 Core Limited Edition is compatible with our free companion app, The Metronome App by Soundbrenner. We’ve just launched an exciting new update on iOS, with some highly requested features like incremental tempo change and muted beats trainer. 

Take your music practice to the stars

To reserve a spot and get your hands on a unit before it sells out, register here. The 2022 Core Limited Edition will be released on July 15th 2022 at a special launch price of $245 reduced from $299.

items.title: Exciting updates on The Metronome App by Soundbrenner
items.editorNotes: Revised for clarity and readability while maintaining factual accuracy.
items.excerptBefore: Train your inner rhythm with the muted beats trainer and challenge yourself with accelerando with The Metronome App by Soundbrenner!
items.excerptAfter: Discover the latest updates to The Metronome App by Soundbrenner, including new features for all users and exclusive options for Soundbrenner Plus subscribers.
items.seoTitleBefore: Exciting updates on The Metronome App by Soundbrenner
items.seoTitleAfter: Exciting Updates on The Metronome App by Soundbrenner
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Train your inner rhythm with the muted beats trainer and challenge yourself with accelerando with The Metronome App by Soundbrenner!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore the new features in The Metronome App by Soundbrenner, including a playback counter, stopwatch, and exclusive features for Soundbrenner Plus…
items.beforeBody:
Musicians, we’re thrilled to share with you our latest updates to The Metronome App by Soundbrenner (version 1.30.0). In fact, these new features are the top suggestions requested by you! This app currently available on iOS only, Android will launch later this year. 

What's new in The Metronome app by Soundbrenner?

New features for all app users

Playback counter
Get organized for your next practice or performance and set up the metronome to automatically pause at the end of the song. Count by the number of bars or by duration of the song.

Stopwatch
Is “practice 30 minutes daily” part of your new year’s resolutions? Achieve your goals and keep track of how long you practice with the metronome! 

Additional features for Soundbrenner Plus subscribers

Muted beats trainer
Want to level up on your inner sense of rhythm? Now you can with the muted beats trainer. Simply set the metronome to periodically mute as you play, and see if you can stay on the beat.

Incremental tempo change
Challenge yourself with our new Accelerando feature! Set an incremental BPM change as you play, either by bar or by time. The only question is, can you keep up?

What is Soundbrenner Plus?

When you subscribe to Soundbrenner Plus, your contribution goes towards adding new, often-requested features to the app, improving the existing ones and offering reliability you can count on. Your subscription funds better music tools that the world urgently needs. And thanks to this paid subscription, we’re able to keep the basic functions of our app free for everyone, to make music-making even more accessible. Currently, we’re at 7 million downloads and counting for our app!

Not a Soundbrenner Plus subscriber, but curious to try out these features?  All users can access Plus for 14 days free of charge. Soundbrenner Pulse or Core 2 owners get free access for 3 months. If you decide to subscribe, you can cancel your subscription anytime if you decide it’s not for you.

What's new in The Metronome app by Soundbrenner?

New features for all app users

Playback counter

Get organized for your next practice or performance by setting up the metronome to automatically pause at the end of the song. Count by the number of bars or by the duration of the song.

Stopwatch

Is “practice 30 minutes daily” part of your New Year’s resolutions? Achieve your goals and keep track of your practice time with the metronome!

Additional features for Soundbrenner Plus subscribers

Muted beats trainer

Want to enhance your inner sense of rhythm? Use the muted beats trainer. Set the metronome to periodically mute while you play and see if you can stay on the beat.

Incremental tempo change

Challenge yourself with our new Accelerando feature! Set an incremental BPM change as you play, either by bar or by time. The only question is, can you keep up?

When you subscribe to Soundbrenner Plus, your contribution helps add new, often-requested features to the app, improve existing ones, and ensure reliability. Unique instruments: Our top 5 picks in the world of music
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original facts and links.
items.excerptBefore: Explore the enchanting world of music with these top 5 unique instruments that unveil the sounds of rarity and redefine musical creativity.
items.excerptAfter: Explore our top 5 unique instruments from around the world, including the Nyckelharpa, Playtron, Hydraulophone, Glass Armonica, and Erhu. Discover their fascinating sounds and histories!
items.seoTitleBefore: Unique instruments: Our top 5 picks in the world of music
items.seoTitleAfter: Top 5 Unique Instruments in Music
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Explore the enchanting world of music with these top 5 unique instruments that unveil the sounds of rarity and redefine musical creativity.
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Discover the top 5 unique instruments from around the world, including the Nyckelharpa, Playtron, Hydraulophone, Glass Armonica, and Erhu. Explore their…
items.beforeBody:
Today, we're embarking on a journey to uncover the top 5 most unique instruments from around the globe – the kind that will leave you utterly amazed and your ears craving for more.

So, let's plunge into a realm of musical creativity that's truly a captivating harmony of originality!

1. Nyckelharpa

Source: Other Minds

From the frosty realms of Sweden come this unique instrument that has been around for more than 600 years yet remains refreshingly novel to many. The Nyckelharpa, which means "keyed fiddle," beautifully marries strings with wooden keys. As these keys get pressed, they stop the strings, resulting in a haunting, resonant sound. Coupled with its intricate wooden design, it's both a pleasure for the eyes and the ears.

Buy now

2. Playtron

The Playtron MIDI Controller offers a unique way to create your custom MIDI controller. It can connect up to 16 objects with alligator clips. This allows you to use it with several mobile apps, online synthesizers, or your favorite DAW.

From fruits to plants, and even toys, the possibilities are endless. Connect the clips from objects capable of conducting electricity to Playtron, and assign a different MIDI note (C1 to D#2 by default) to each object. Then, you can start playing and creating your unique sounds!

Buy now

3. Hydraulophone

Source: Research Gate

Diving deep into innovation, we encounter the Hydraulophone – an instrument played by manipulating water flow. The instrument's pipes are designed to emit different pitches when water is pressed through them. Found in places like science centers and interactive museums, the Hydraulophone is a unique instrument that lets you literally touch the music. It's a symphony of water and sound, offering a refreshing take on musical expression.

Learn more

4. Glass Armonica: 

Source: Benjamin Franklin House

Invented by Benjamin Franklin. It is a mesmerizing and unique instrument made up of spinning glass bowls of varying sizes. By wetting your fingers and gently touching the glass, you can create beautiful and crystal-clear tones. With a history dating back to the 18th century, this instrument continues to bewitch listeners with its delicate and soothing melodies.

Buy now

5. Erhu: 

Source: Cultural Keys

Lastly, from the vast landscapes of China, emerges the Erhu. This two-stringed unique instrument, often referred to as the Chinese violin, has a sound that tugs directly at the heartstrings. Despite its simple construction, the Erhu can convey a broad spectrum of emotions, from the depths of sorrow to the amazing heights of joy.

Buy now

Bonus Pick!

Djembe

Source: Marshall Music

Next, let's journey to the heart of West Africa, where the rhythmic beats of the Djembe reverberate. This goblet-shaped drum is made of a single piece of wood and topped with animal skin. 

When played, it produces a range of sounds, from deep base tones to sharp, high slaps. Traditionally, this unique instrument has been central to ceremonies, stories, and celebrations, echoing the rich tapestry of African culture.

Buy now

Wrapping up

These five unique instruments are proof of human creativity and our endless pursuit of new ways to produce beautiful sounds. They challenge the conventional boundaries of music and invite us to explore musical tunes, that redefine the essence of musical art.

If you really want to master these instruments, then try training with a Soundbrenner metronome Pulse, which is the perfect gadget to get your tunes and rhythms flowing. Let your ears be your guide as you embark on an auditory adventure like no other.

PS. If you like this blog on unique instruments, we are sure our blog on the top 6 weird instruments will also intrigue you. Check it out here!

items.title: 8 tips to promote your band in 2025
items.editorNotes: Rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original content's meaning.
items.excerptBefore: These eight highly effective marketing strategies will give you ideas on how to promote your band and grow your fan base in no time!
items.excerptAfter: Discover eight effective strategies to promote your band in 2025, from leveraging streaming platforms to collaborating with other artists.
items.seoTitleBefore: 8 tips to promote your band in 2025
items.seoTitleAfter: 8 Tips to Promote Your Band in 2025
items.seoDescriptionBefore: These eight highly effective marketing strategies will give you ideas on how to promote your band and grow your fan base in no time!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Learn how to effectively promote your band in 2025 with these eight essential marketing strategies, including social media, live shows, and content…
items.beforeBody:
There are over 24,000 professional musicians trying to make a name for themselves in the U.S., and that doesn’t even count those who don’t write “musician” on their tax forms. It’s not easy to get a start as a band. But by using these eight highly effective marketing strategies, you’ll be playing sold-out shows to screaming fans in no time.

1. Join Spotify for artists

The music industry in 2025 is all about streaming. If you want to get more listeners, get your music on a streaming platform.

Luckily, one of the largest streaming platforms in the world is also one of the easiest for bands to get their music featured. Spotify has over 406 million active monthly users, and they make it easy for bands to add their music using the Spotify for Artists dashboard.

Just sign up, upload your music, and start building your audience. You can also submit music directly to Spotify curation teams to get your songs featured on other popular playlists and grow your fanbase even more. If you get enough plays every month, you could even make some money!


2. Work with a manager or promoter

You’re a musician, not a marketing mentor. And even if you are a master of marketing, you can’t spend all your time on marketing because you have to make new music. That’s why it’s helpful to hire a manager to handle your marketing strategies to promote your band.

It might cost some money, but working with a manager or promoter to promote your band is a great way to continue building your audience without taking your focus away from the music. Let someone else handle the promotion, so you can work on creating new and exciting tunes for your fans.

3. Harness the power of social media

Social media is a band’s best friend in the modern age. Every time you play a killer show, you’ll get more and more followers to your page. Even better, everyone who follows your page already likes your music, so every time you play a show or release new music, you can make a post or two on your social media feed and instantly get the word out!

However, creating an effective social media marketing strategy is more than just scheduling a post now and then. You have to create regular, high-quality content to promote your band. Remember, social media is huge. Over half the world’s population is on social media! You have to compete with a lot of other bands and content creators for attention. Make sure your content is just as good—if not better—than theirs.


You should also schedule posts regularly. Attention spans are short in 2025, and you don’t want your fans to forget about you! Posting about three to five times per week is considered ideal for most social media marketing campaigns.

While that might sound like a lot of content, it doesn’t all have to be music. Show your band’s personality with fun behind-the-scenes videos or even gear rundowns. Anything to keep your band front-and-center in your fans’ minds.

The main downside of social media is that your reach is limited to those who follow you. Depending on your budget, it wouldn’t hurt to boost posts or create social media ads. They’re an inexpensive way to extend your band’s marketing reach beyond just your followers, and hopefully pick up some new fans in the process.

4. Play shows

Live shows are easily one of the best ways to promote your band. It has been true for decades, and it’s still true in 2025. The energy and emotion of a live show is a guaranteed way to gain new fans, even if they might not have initially been interested in your sound.

If you want to grow your fan base, play as many shows as you can! People can’t resist a good performance. One good show and you can increase your band’s following by dozens or hundreds of people.


Plus, you never know who might be in the audience. There could be an influencer with millions of social media followers, or even a record label executive looking for the next big act. Even a good small show could make your band blow up in ways you didn’t expect.

Keep playing shows and try to level up your performance each time. Try to get bigger venues, play with more popular bands, and put on higher-level productions. 

It’s also a good idea to play outside your hometown. Yes, it’s a great town, but there are fans farther away too who are looking for your sound!

5. Go old-school with concert flyers

Of course, live shows are only an effective marketing method if people actually show up. That’s why it’s important to promote your shows.

Even though we might be in the digital age, there’s still no substitute for the classic concert flyer. A well-designed concert poster can attract tons of attention on the street and around town. You don’t have to get followers or email addresses; passersby simply see the poster, think how cool it looks, and put your next show in their calendars.

If you’re not a designer, that’s okay. There are tons of concert flyer templates online to help. Just find the template that works best for your band, fill in the information, and download the completed flyer. You’re ready to hit the print shop and start distributing right away. You can also post the flyers to your social media channels for a little extra marketing efficiency!

6. Start a YouTube channel

You might think Spotify or Apple Music are the biggest music streaming services on the internet, but they’re tiny compared to the true music giant on the block.

While other streaming services are lucky if they hit a couple of hundred million subscribers, YouTube Music is sitting with a comfortable lead at a whopping 2 billion active music subscribers! If you want to grow your band’s audience base, start a YouTube channel.

YouTube is great for all kinds of content. You’re not just stuck with recorded music. Show your fans who your band really is by posting various forms of content:

  • Music videos
  • Backstage shenanigans
  • Practice sessions
  • Songwriting processes
  • Live shows

The key to a successful YouTube channel (or any social media channel, for that matter) is to post high-quality content. Use a good camera with audio capabilities. Your iPhone’s mic won’t capture your band’s sound as well as a quality external mic. If you want more fans, you have to do it right!


7. Collaborate with other artists

Want a quick and fun way to extend your fan base to people who otherwise wouldn’t have heard your band? Collaborate with other artists or musicians.

When you collaborate with another band, you get access to their entire fan base. And, if you do a good job, they’ll become your fans too!

Not only is collaborating with other artists a great marketing tactic, but it can also help you create new and exciting content. Other bands might think differently about the creative process than you do. When you work together with someone else, you can blend your styles to boost creativity and create music you’d otherwise not be able to.

8. Release regular content

At the end of the day, the best way to promote your band in 2025 is to release regular content. Whether it’s via social media, releasing songs on your Spotify channel, or playing new songs live, new content is what keeps your current fans around and engages new fans.


Never stop writing and performing new music. When you become complacent, so do your fans. Continue to release quality content regularly to keep your fans active and engaged with your band. That’s the only way to gain traction and grow your fanbase.

Tell the world about your band.

Your band makes great music, and it’s about time it was heard by the world! The best way to market your band in 2025 is to take advantage of the various digital channels available, play plenty of live shows, collaborate with other musicians, and hire a producer or manager. But the most effective thing you can do to promote your band is to release regular content to keep your music fresh and relevant.

2025 is your year. Follow these tips, and you’ll be playing sold-out shows to screaming fans in no time! 

If you enjoyed this article, check out our “How to prep for live music & gigs” guide!

items.title: What is the difference between rhythm and beat?
items.editorNotes: The article has been rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original meaning and factual content.
items.excerptBefore: Musical terminology can trip up even experienced musicians. Learn how rhythm and beat differ in musical lingo and ways you can improve both.
items.excerptAfter: Explore the differences between rhythm and beat in music, and learn how understanding these concepts can enhance your musical skills and appreciation.
items.seoTitleBefore: What is the difference between rhythm and beat?
items.seoTitleAfter: Understanding Rhythm and Beat in Music
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Musical terminology can confuse even experienced musicians. Learn how rhythm and beat differ in musical lingo and ways to improve both
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Learn the key differences between rhythm and beat in music, and how mastering these concepts can improve your musical skills and appreciation.
items.beforeBody:
Music is any combination of sounds meant to express art or sentiment. Birds chirping, stick hitting a drum, it’s all music. Playing music, however, requires one to differentiate between all the layers of sound and the elements that come together to compose a piece of music. 

Learning musical terminologies like rhythm, beat, tempo, and time can help you grow your skill as a musician. Knowing the difference between these will also help you appreciate great music and play it better.

Learning the difference between rhythm and beat

We can have long debates on how rhythm and beat differ, and what parallels we can draw between the two. 

In the simplest of terms, rhythm is the basic pattern or grid in which musical notes move and flow. The beat is the tempo that remains consistent throughout the piece. Check out these Rhythm Basics to learn more about the components that make up music.

The two can be confusing because they are not entirely separate. The beat is often taken for the rhythm and vice versa. That’s because the beat is part of the rhythm of any musical piece.

What is beat?

The beat makes up the harmonic or melodic elements of a song. It's the continual, unwavering motion that the rest of the song is built on. It can be easily distinguished and sets the timing to which we clap along or stamp our feet.

If you’ve ever wondered if those who have partial hearing loss can enjoy music, then beat is your answer. They can hear the vibrations of a song's beat if it’s played loud enough. 

The beat is also the cue that keeps all musicians playing their instruments in perfect timing with the song and each other. It’s what orchestrates the elements of music, making the song cohesive and well-fitting. 

Most of the timing and structure of a song is made of its beat, which makes it very important that musicians stay on the beat when playing. The Core’s vibrating metronome can help you keep up with the beat effortlessly and develop your internal rhythm.

What is rhythm?

Rhythm can be best described as the repetitive pattern of beats and timing in a song. It’s the binding medium that adheres the components of a song together. Rhythm is also what decides the placement and repetition of these components.

The beat is the bare skeleton structure of the song. Rhythm is how the song is built on that structure. It weaves together all the other musical elements of the song to give it a unique signature.

Rhythm can change within a piece of music, but the beat remains consistent. It's also the main differentiating factor between different musical genres. 

Master the basics of rhythm and beat now

Educating yourself about the basics of rhythm and beat is the best way to play well-synchronized music. Remember, mastering theory and practice are both hallmarks of a truly great musician.

So what are you waiting for? Get yourself this top-rated and free Metronome app today to start your musical journey the right way. 

Happy practicing!

items.excerptBefore: A melody in your mind? Don’t miss these rhythm tips for songwriters that will transform your tune into a masterpiece!
items.excerptAfter: Discover 10 essential tips for songwriters to improve rhythm and create captivating music.
items.seoTitleBefore: Tips for songwriters | 10 ways to improve your rhythm
items.seoTitleAfter: 10 Tips for Songwriters to Improve Rhythm
items.seoDescriptionBefore: A melody in your mind? Don’t miss these rhythm tips for songwriters that will transform your tune into a masterpiece!
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore these 10 essential tips for songwriters to enhance rhythm and create memorable music. Perfect for both novice and experienced musicians.
items.beforeBody:
Ready to turn those hums and strums into your next hit? Here are our top 10 tips for songwriters to improve rhythm.

Let's straight away start with rhythm - the heartbeat of any song. Whether you’re a novice songwriter or a seasoned pro, rhythm is essential for creating that catchy, memorable tune. 

In this blog post, we’ll share 10 rhythm tips for songwriters to help your music flow smoothly and captivate your audience. 

So, grab your instrument, and let’s get started! 🎵🎶

1. Start with a strong beat

The beat is the foundation of your song. Start by creating a strong, consistent beat that supports the mood and style of your song. Use a metronome, like the Soundbrenner Core 2, to keep your timing tight.

If you're into drumming or work closely with drummers, understanding their tools, like the best double bass pedals, can also enhance the rhythm quality. [Link to "7 Best Double Bass Pedals: A Comprehensive Roundup in 2023"] Use a metronome, like the Soundbrenner Core 2, to keep your timing tight.

2. Experiment with different time signatures

Don’t limit yourself to the common 4/4 time signature. Experiment with different time signatures like 3/4, 6/8, or 7/8 to give your song a unique flavor.

3. Use dynamics

Dynamics refers to the volume of your music. Use variations in dynamics to add interest and emotion to your song.

Play around with:

  • Louder and softer sections
  • Gradual increases or decreases in volume

4. Vary your rhythm patterns

Variation is key to keeping your audience engaged. Mix up your rhythm patterns throughout your song to create contrast and excitement.

5. Syncopation is your friend

Syncopation involves emphasizing the offbeats or weaker beats in a measure. It’s a great way to add a little unexpected twist to your rhythm.

6. Use rests wisely

Rests are as important as notes. Use them to create space and tension in your music.

7. Understand subdivisions

Subdivisions are smaller note values that fit within a beat. Understanding subdivisions will help you create more intricate rhythms and make your music more interesting.

8. Practice with a metronome

Practicing with a metronome will help you develop your sense of timing and rhythm. The Soundbrenner Pulse is a wearable metronome that vibrates to the beat, helping you internalize the rhythm.

9. Listen and learn

Listen to different genres of music and pay attention to the rhythm. Analyze what works and what doesn’t, and apply these insights to your own music.

10. Record yourself

Recording yourself playing is a great way to identify any rhythm issues and areas for improvement.

Conclusion

Rhythm is the soul of any song, and as a songwriter, it’s essential to give it the attention it deserves. Practice makes perfect, so don’t be afraid to experiment and try new things. 

With these tips for songwriters, you’ll be well on your way to creating rhythmically engaging and memorable music.

So, what are you waiting for? Get out there and make some noise!

items.title: 10 creative ways to incorporate music into our daily lives
items.editorNotes: The article was rewritten for clarity and readability while maintaining the original meaning and factual content.
items.excerptBefore: Discover the 10 best ways of incorporating music in our daily lives and use it to make our everyday routine more enjoyable and rhythmic,
items.excerptAfter: Discover ten creative ways to incorporate music into your daily life, from songwriting challenges to musical DIY projects.
items.seoTitleBefore: 10 creative ways to incorporate music into our daily lives
items.seoTitleAfter: 10 Creative Ways to Incorporate Music into Daily Life
items.seoDescriptionBefore: Discover the 10 best ways of incorporating music in our daily lives and use it to make our everyday routine more enjoyable and rhythmic,
items.seoDescriptionAfter: Explore unique ideas to add music to your everyday routine, including songwriting challenges, musical DIY projects, and more.
items.beforeBody:
We all know the usual ways to incorporate music into our daily lives, but today, we’re sharing ten truly creative and unique ideas. 

Let's make our everyday music more exciting!

1. Songwriting challenges

Start a songwriting challenge with your friends or social media followers. Pick a theme and challenge each other to write a song or verse about it. 

Share your creations and see how different minds interpret the same theme! This activity not only promotes creativity but also helps in improving your songwriting skills. It is the perfect way of adding music to our daily lives as we hang out with our friends and family on a daily basis.

2. Musical DIY projects

Create your own musical instruments using household items. A water bottle maraca, a cereal box guitar, or even a homemade drum set! There are endless possibilities to get creative and musical. 

This is also a great activity for kids to learn about music and recycling at the same time. It allows the addition of music in our daily lives while keeping the essence of learning alive.

3. Lyrics scavenger hunt

Create a scavenger hunt using song lyrics as clues. Participants will have to guess the song and artist to find the next clue and, ultimately, the treasure! 

This is a fun and interactive way to test your and your friends' knowledge about different songs and artists, and an exciting way of adding music to our daily lives.

4. Musical plant care

Did you know that plants respond positively to music? 

Play music for your plants and observe the changes. It’s a win-win situation: your plants thrive, and you have a reason to play music all day! 

This is also a great experiment to teach kids about plant growth and the effects of sound on living organisms.

5. Singing messages 

Instead of sending regular text messages, try sending voice notes of you singing the messages, adding a hint of music to our daily lives. 

It will bring a smile to the receiver’s face and make communication more fun! And it's a fun way to practice your singing skills.

6. Soundtrack your day

Create a soundtrack for your day with songs that match your activities and mood. Share it with friends and compare soundtracks to see how different or similar your days are! 

This activity will make you more mindful of your daily activities and how music influences your mood.

7. Music and drawing

Listen to a song and draw whatever it inspires in you. It could be an abstract drawing, a portrait, or even a comic strip! This activity helps in improving your listening skills and allows you to express your feelings through art. It also promotes the incorporation of music into our daily lives.

8. Music and cooking

This one is not just about dancing while cooking. Try to create a recipe inspired by a song or an artist. 

For example, a "Strawberry Fields Forever" salad or a "Rolling in the Deep" fried chicken. 

This activity will make cooking more fun and creative.

9. Desk drumming

Turn your desk into a drum set using pencils, notebooks, and other stationery items. 

The Soundbrenner Pulse, a wearable metronome, can help you keep the rhythm while you drum away! 

This activity will help you take a break from work and release some stress.

10. Music-themed parties

Host music-themed parties. For example, a '70s disco party, a musical karaoke night, or a '90s grunge party. Encourage your guests to dress up and bring their favorite songs from that era. This activity will make your parties more fun and memorable.

Conclusion 

Music in our daily lives can be more than just background noise. From songwriting challenges to musical DIY projects, there are plenty of unique and creative ways to incorporate music into your daily routine. 

What are some other ways you add music to your day? Let us know in the comments below, and don’t forget to check out the Soundbrenner Core 2 to help keep your rhythm during your desk drumming sessions.
